content.explanation:
**Flowers & Fruits Vocabulary — फूल और फल:**

**FLOWERS (फूल):**
| English | Hindi |
|---------|-------|
| Rose | गुलाब |
| Lotus | कमल |
| Jasmine | चमेली |
| Marigold | गेंदा |
| Sunflower | सूरजमुखी |
| Lavender | लैवेंडर |
| Hibiscus | गुड़हल |
| Tulip | ट्यूलिप |
| Lily | कुमुद |
| Orchid | आर्किड |

**FRUITS (फल):**
| English | Hindi |
|---------|-------|
| Mango | आम |
| Pomegranate | अनार |
| Guava | अमरूद |
| Jackfruit | कटहल |
| Papaya | पपीता |
| Custard apple | सीताफल |
| Watermelon | तरबूज़ |
| Litchi | लीची |
| Sapota/Chikoo | चीकू |
| Pineapple | अनानास |

**Describing Flowers & Fruits:**
Fragrant (सुगंधित), blooming (खिला हुआ), ripe (पका हुआ), juicy (रसीला), seedless (बीज रहित)
rules: Flowers and fruits are countable: a rose, two mangoes, several flowers
Fragrant, beautiful, colorful = adjectives for flowers
Ripe, sweet, juicy, fresh = adjectives for fruits
Verb + flower: flowers bloom, wilt, grow, spread fragrance
Verb + fruit: fruits ripen, fall, grow, harvest
"A bunch of flowers" / "A basket of fruits" = collective expressions
"In season" = जब मिलता है: "Mangoes are in season now."
content.memoryTrick:
**Flowers BLOOM 🌸, Fruits RIPEN 🍎**
Fragrant flowers = चमेली, गुलाब (sweet smell)
Sour fruit = imli, nimbu | Sweet fruit = आम, लीची
National flower = Lotus 🪷 | National fruit = Mango 🥭

examples.hindi: बगीचे में गुलाब खिले हुए हैं।
examples.english: The roses are blooming in the garden.
The lotus is the national flower.
Mango is the king of fruits.

examples.hindi: चमेली के फूल बहुत सुगंधित होते हैं।
अनार खाने से खून बढ़ता है।

mistakes.wrong: I bought a flower bouquet of roses.
mistakes.correct: I bought a bouquet of roses.
mistakes.why: 'Bouquet' already means bunch of flowers — no need to add 'flower'

mistakes.correct: Mango is my favourite fruit. / Mangoes are my favourite fruit.
mistakes.why: If using general plural 'mangoes', verb = 'are'; if singular idea, use 'mango is'
After 'smell' (linking verb), use adjective (fragrant), not adverb

mistakes.wrong: I want to eat ripe mangos.
mistakes.correct: I want to eat ripe mangoes.
mistakes.why: Plural of mango = mangoes (not mangos — both acceptable but 'mangoes' preferred)

speakingTips: Gift: 'I brought you a bouquet of fresh roses and lilies.'
Cultural: 'In India, marigold flowers are used in almost every celebration.'
Seasonal: 'Summer is the best time because mangoes and litchis are in season.'

vocabulary.example: Bees help in the pollination of flowers.
The garden was filled with the fragrance of jasmine.
The flowers wilted in the heat.

practice.english: The rose is a symbol of love.

practice.hindi: आम भारत का राष्ट्रीय फल है।
practice.english: Mango is the national fruit of India.
alternatives: the national fruit of india is mango.
practice.hint: mango = आम, national fruit = राष्ट्रीय फल

practice.hindi: सेब खाने से डॉक्टर दूर रहता है।
practice.english: An apple a day keeps the doctor away.
alternatives: eating an apple daily keeps you healthy.
the national flower of india is the lotus.

practice.hindi: वह बगीचे में फूल लगाती है।
practice.english: She plants flowers in the garden.
alternatives: she grows flowers in her garden.

practice.hindi: अंगूर का रस बहुत मीठा होता है।
सूरजमुखी सूरज की तरफ मुड़ता है।
practice.english: The sunflower turns towards the sun.

practice.hindi: पपीता पेट के लिए अच्छा है।
practice.english: Papaya is good for the stomach.

practice.hindi: चमेली की महक बहुत अच्छी होती है।
practice.english: Jasmine has a very pleasant fragrance.

practice.hindi: अनार का जूस स्वास्थ्य के लिए फायदेमंद है।
practice.english: Pomegranate juice is beneficial for health.
alternatives: pomegranate juice is good for your health.

practice.hindi: गेंदे का फूल बहुत आम है।
practice.english: The marigold flower is very common.

practice.hindi: स्ट्रॉबेरी देखने में लाल होती है।
लीची का मौसम गर्मियों में होता है।
